schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The first real device on the spine — the system's notary and its proving mechanism (MAP.md:569). `TesterDevice` subclasses `BaseDevice` (so it carries CP1-CP6 and reports the Form v0 #2 surface). Its capabilities today: (1) `run_proof(path)` — run a proof file as a subprocess and PRODUCE a VALIDATION record (the ratified eight fields: claim, caller, date, method, verdict, evidence, falsifier, horizon); the verdict is read from the subject's exit code, not granted. (2) It OWNS THE NETWORK (MAP.md:333): `run_proof(..., isolation='netns')` runs the subject inside a MEASURED network seal (`cairn/tester/isolation.py`) — the seal is probed from inside (never assumed) and its verdict is folded into the record's `method`+`evidence`, no ninth field. An unconfirmable seal is INDETERMINATE (CP1) and never launders into a green.
why: Law 8 — nothing enters proven-space without a proof a hollow build couldn't pass; the verdict must come from a hand the builder did not guide. Placing that gate in a device (not a convention each builder self-applies) is Law 4 (physics over policy): the proving and the attesting come from the same hand, so a green cannot be self-granted. This is the smallest honest version of the tester — it proves, it attests, it dogfoods (its first act validates the real base proofs) — while the pillars it will grow (network ownership, a durable store, richer verdict methods) stay filed edges, built when a real need pulls them (round-three discipline: simplest provable stone first).
invoke: import: `from cairn.tester.device import TesterDevice`. Prove: `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_tester.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — the first runtime component on the spine (base/ is the code-seam it stands on).
entry: FRESH design, MECHANISM grafted. The tester's design — device-hood, the VALIDATION record, the seal's four verdicts mapped to CP1/Law 3, the seal recorded INSIDE the VALIDATION rather than a parallel record — is Cairn's, authored to Form v0 (like device.py/shim.py). The seal's OS PLUMBING crosses nearly literally from UU's isolation.py (the `bwrap --unshare-net --cap-add CAP_NET_ADMIN` flag string, the inside-probe, the `available()` apparmor check) because it is kernel truth, not a design Cairn replaced. What did NOT cross: UU's netpolicy Router (fixture/refuse/forward + the nftables reject+counter) — the programmable-network pillar, deferred (see open edge (b)); and UU's verdict shape (seal_confirmed/seal_detail/duration_s), replaced by the Cairn VALIDATION.
state: PROVED for the minimal stone: (proving) TesterDevice runs a proof as a subprocess and produces a well-formed VALIDATION; a KNOWN-GREEN fixture validates green and a KNOWN-RED fixture validates red (the hollow-TESTER killer — verified by falsification: an always-green tester trips test_red_proof_validates_red; a nine-field record trips the schema tooth). (network) It runs a subject inside a MEASURED network seal; on this host the seal is LIVE-CONFIRMED (1.1.1.1:53 reachable bare, unreachable inside netns), and the hollow-SEAL killer is verified by falsification (a netns whose wrap() removes no route is caught BREACHED, not trusted; an always-SEALED measurement fails the four-verdict proof). It is the FIRST real subject the base's armed composition trap bites on. Proven green, no install. OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) VALIDATIONS are PRODUCED, not yet PERSISTED — the durable greppable store is db_domain's stone (CLAUDE.md pending rule); this device returns records and writes nothing, so class-space stays state-free; (b) the seal delivers NO ROUTE (the closed half); the CHOSEN ROUTE — a Router that serves a FIXTURE, REFUSES with a kernel counter, or FORWARDS over a Unix socket for a named dependency — is deferred, pulled by db_domain (FORWARD to Postgres, preserving peer auth) and inference_domain (FIXTURE/REFUSE). This advances but does NOT retire CLAUDE.md's 'reached only through the domain' IOUs: they retire when the domain is the SOLE route AND a kernel rule / import-scan enforces it; (c) the verdict METHOD is exit-code + seal only — quorum / review-by-N-experts for concept pieces (MAP.md:752) is a later method the `method` field names room for; (d) reported-vs-filed intention drift detection is not wired.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_tester.py, runnable bare (python3) today; the tester will eventually own how proofs run, including its own. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) `run_proof` on a passing proof yields anything but green, or on a failing proof anything but red (the notary must not lie); (2) a VALIDATION record's field-set is not exactly the ratified eight (drift — extra or missing field), including when the seal rides inside it; (3) `TesterDevice` stops subclassing `BaseDevice` or drops CP1-CP6 (trips the base's composition trap); (4) `introspect()` stops reporting intention -> state -> settings in order; (5) a seal claimed present (isolation='netns') but measured to still hold a route is called anything but BREACHED — a sandbox that reports the shape of a seal while handing out live sockets is the exact defect the tester exists to kill.
how_it_learns: Yes — and it is where learning-from-proof concentrates. Every VALIDATION carries falsifier + horizon (Law 3), so each verdict knows how it expires — 'validated on the 17th' can itself go stale and be re-run; a verdict is a best-guess-with-an-expiry, not a permanent stamp. A red is not a failure to suppress but CP2 data (FAIL = Further Advance In Learning), kicked back to design. THE SEAL LEARNS THE SAME WAY: it is measured from inside every run, never assumed, so a misconfigured or silently-downgraded sandbox is CAUGHT rather than trusted; a seal that cannot be confirmed is INDETERMINATE (CP1 made structural — 'I don't know' is a legible verdict, not a hidden green). The component's own horizon: its verdict METHOD is provisional — exit-code+seal today, quorum / review-by-experts for concept pieces later (MAP.md:752); the seal itself will grow from NO-ROUTE to a CHOSEN route (Router) when db/inference domains pull it. Once db_domain gives VALIDATIONS a durable home, that store is the evidence trail that turns answered proofs into structure (Law 1) instead of re-derivation — a mind greps it on a hunch rather than re-proving.
traces_to: Law 8 (a proof a hollow build couldn't pass — the tester IS that gate)
Law 4 (physics over policy — proving + attesting from one hand)
Law 3 (a VALIDATION expires: falsifier + horizon ride along)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
Form v0 #2 (the ordered introspection surface, MAP.md:157)
owner: CC owns the tester's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the VALIDATION schema's ratification (MAP.md:569) and the node-class definitions.
proof: proofs/test_tester.py — device-hood + the six, Form v0 #2 order, green->green, red->red (the hollow-tester killer), state reflects runs, exercises the base's armed trap; plus a __main__ dogfood that validates the real base proofs. proofs/test_isolation.py — the network seal: the four seal verdicts measured (incl. the hollow-seal BREACHED case), a LIVE bwrap seal on a capable host (honest INDETERMINATE otherwise), and run_proof folds the seal into the 8 fields with no schema drift. proofs/fixtures/ — a green (exit 0) and a red (exit 1) proof. Green: `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_tester.py` and `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_isolation.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-17 as the third step of the code spine (base -> base pair -> tester), the first real DEVICE. Same day, grew its OTHER half — the kernel-owned network's measured SEAL (cairn/tester/isolation.py) — completing the 'tester + kernel-owned network' spine step's no-route half (MAP.md:888). Still deferred until a real need pulls them: the durable VALIDATIONS store (db_domain), the chosen-route Router (db/inference domains), and richer verdict methods.
